notes: Installed it with Chinese as Steam language and almost all ingame text was replaced with boxes. I was able to fix most but not all in-game texts by running WINEPREFIX=~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/221380/pfx/ winetricks , choosing Wine Default Prefix and then installing a couple of chinese fonts. Providing CJK fonts to the wine prefixes at installation time, might improve the out of the box experience though.

notes:
For me the game worked with Proton 3.16-8 Beta (after applying the AoK HD.exe -> Launcher.exe). With Proton 4.2-1 and no AoK HD name change the game just doesn't start. If I change AoK HD.exe -> Launcher.exe, the game still doesn't start, but it fails with a pop-up saying: "Game failed to initialize Steam. You must have Steam running."
So for the time being, until I find some solution I will have to revert back to Proton 3.16-8 Beta where the game works, only the font in multiplayer chat windows is completely borked and can't be read. I was hoping Proton 4.2-1 might solve that but unfortunatelly, for me it doesn't work at all.

notes: Despite the database flagging my drivers in red, ProtonDB isn't equipped to understand these are the latest drivers for my video card.  The game will not launch, even after tweaking it with the "Launcher.exe" rename.  I have also tried the previous versions of Proton, but to no avail.  Without any modifications beyond the "Launcher.exe" rename, WINE plays it just fine, on my current system.  Note that my WINE install is in a completely separate location from my Linux+Proton install.

title: Age of Empires II HD

notes:
Tested:

Single player and multiplayer.

Issues:

After installing the game, single player worked. Contrary to what I've read before, and what happens on Windows, there was no crash or hang during startup or when listing the workshop items.
However, starting a multiplayer game caused the screen to constantly scroll to the left.

Text in text entry fields is invisible by default, flickers fast when typing.

Tweaks:

After exiting Steam and renaming "AoK HD.exe" to "Launcher.exe" as recommended by many, multiplayer also worked perfectly.

Text entry still does not work well. This might put people off. To me, it is acceptable.

Performance:

Superb, better than the original on Windows.
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes: Just works now
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes: click on workshop after the first time you run it in order to get past workshop verification. After that it runs smoothly out of the box
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes:
After playing single and multiplayer several times, I have to degrade my rating from gold to silver. Although the first issue of the screen scrolling to the left has not come up again, there was once an issue that after going into windowed mode, I could not click anything any more. Also, there is no way to use the window controls or move the window. Alt + Drag on Linux also does not work, although that might be unavoidable due to using Wine.
With issues like that creeping up from time to time, it might put off a lot of people.
title: Age of Empires II HD

title: Age of Empires II HD

notes:
I upgrade my rating from silver to gold (again):
1. issue: Screen scrolls in one direction after window loses focus.
Workaround: I disabled the hotkeys for screen scrolling, which were the arrow keys by default.
2. issue: Text invisible/filckering in text entry fields: I installed a couple of drivers and libraries in a failed attempt to Battle.net with Lutris, and now text entry is perfect!
3. issue: Window handling. There are a lot of small issues with windowed mode, but at least after clicking the top border, it is possible to drag the window once, before the mouse is locked again.
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes: After updating libraries for Lutris and Battle.net, text no longer flickers in entry fields. Window handling is inconsistent, but not too bad. 1 click is required in windowed mode at the top border to be able to drag the window. Workaround for scrolling lock: disabled hotkeys for screen scrolling.
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes: did not work with proton 3.7, works with 4.2
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes:
Rating upgraded from silver to gold.
The text flickering issue was fixed in the update from Proton 4.2-2 to 4.2-3 .
Window handling is still troublesome, but overall manageable. Clicking the top of the window from the game should enable the mouse to drag the window once. Alt + Enter sometimes doesn't react, an Alt + Tab to a different window first might be required.
Workaround for the screen getting stuck scrolling in one direction: disabled the hotkeys for screen scrolling.

notes:
Before starting the game i needed to enable the Beta Client on settings, restart steam and in the installation folder rename AoK.exe to Launcher.exe (Delete the original Launcher.exe before doing that), after that the game was able to start normally, but without these fixes and workarounds it either didn't launch or was stuck 'validating subscription'.

PTBR:
Antes de iniciar o jogo foi necessário habilitar o cliente beta nas configurções, depois que reiniciar a steam, vá no local da instalação e renomeie o AoK.exe para Launcher.exe (Apague o Launcher.exe original antes de fazer isso), após fazer isso o jogo conseguiu iniciar e deu pra jogar sem nenhum problema, antes disso ele não iniciava ou ficava preso numa tela de 'validação de mods'
title: Age of Empires II HD

notes:
Game works fine but the infinite scrolling bug is annoying. To fix it without disabling scrolling hotkeys i installed this patch: https://github.com/kukkerman/aoe2-scroll-bugfix
The patch is compatible with multiplayer.
title: Age of Empires II (2013)

notes:
Bypassing Launcher.exe:
1. game list -> right click on AoE -> properties -> browse local files
2. rename/remove Launcher.exe
3. rename AoK HD.exe to Launcher.exe

Disabling scroll keys:
1. (in-game) options -> hotkeys -> scroll commands
2. change all to "return"
3. game commands
4. change "send chat message" to "return"
